title: Route v2 expansion and support phase
summary: Forty-third structured packet for Eco Explorer. This lane-4 wave extends the outing loop beyond the current forest chapter by first adding a true coastal transect Route v2 type on `scrub-edge-pattern`, then following with one calm support-slot and notebook-return pass built around `note-tabs` plus route-authored filing copy.

execution_order.goal: Prepare the first post-forest Route v2 expansion pack.
execution_order.why_now: The core forest chapter now proves the shape, so the next gain is to bring that authored outing feel into another route family.

execution_order.goal: Implement a true coastal transect Route v2 type and convert `scrub-edge-pattern` on the edge-pattern line to use it.
execution_order.why_now: The next gameplay gain is a route that really teaches staged coast-to-forest reading instead of another unordered evidence set.

execution_order.goal: Review the coastal transect Route v2 expansion pass.
execution_order.why_now: The converted edge-pattern outing should feel like a purposeful mini-transect without cluttering the station shell or reopening the save model.

execution_order.goal: Prepare one calm support-slot and notebook-return follow-on.
execution_order.why_now: Once another route family feels stronger, the next lane-4 gain is a tiny extra choice and a slightly richer filing payoff.

execution_order.goal: Implement `note-tabs`, support-aware strip copy, and richer route-authored filing payoff.
execution_order.why_now: This can make outings feel more complete without opening a larger inventory or quest shell.

execution_order.goal: Review the support-slot and notebook-return follow-on.
execution_order.why_now: The support choice and filing payoff should feel satisfying, not noisy.

guardrails: Do not add a new station page, quest log, or inventory shell.
Keep support growth tiny and optional.
Reuse the existing routeV2Progress.evidenceSlots seam instead of adding another transect save ledger.
Reuse existing world-state and filing seams.
Keep the tone cozy, notebook-first, and science-forward.
Keep the third support text-only and inside the existing `SUPPORT` row plus strip logic.
acceptance_focus: A non-forest route family gains a stronger Route v2 outing shape.
One additional support choice and filing payoff make outings feel more complete without clutter.
Lane 4 remains about clear goals, small choices, and notebook-style return.
handoff_prompt: Read AGENTS.md, .agents/lane-runner.md, .agents/project-memory.md, .agents/work-queue.md, .agents/lanes/lane-4.md, docs/reports/2026-03-31-route-v2-expansion-and-support-phase.md, docs/reports/2026-03-31-note-tabs-notebook-return-handoff.md, and this packet. Treat this as lane 4's next gameplay-loop wave: extend Route v2 beyond the current forest chapter, then add one calm support-slot and notebook-return follow-on without opening a larger quest or inventory shell.
